questions.question: Define a circle.
questions.answer: A circle is the collection of all points in a plane which are at a constant distance from a fixed point called the centre.

questions.question: What is meant by the radius of a circle?
questions.answer: The radius is the constant distance from the centre to any point on the circle.

questions.question: Define a tangent to a circle.
questions.answer: A tangent is a line that touches a circle at exactly one point.

questions.question: What is a secant?
questions.answer: A secant is a line that intersects a circle at two distinct points.

questions.question: What is the point of contact?
questions.answer: The point where a tangent touches the circle is called the point of contact.

questions.question: How many tangents can be drawn at a point on a circle?
questions.answer: Only one tangent can be drawn at a point on a circle.

questions.question: What is a chord of a circle?
questions.answer: A chord is a line segment joining any two points on a circle.

questions.question: What is the diameter of a circle?
questions.answer: The diameter is the longest chord of a circle and is twice the radius.

questions.question: Is every diameter a chord? Give reason.
questions.answer: Yes, because it joins two points on the circle.

questions.question: Define the normal to a circle.
questions.answer: The line containing the radius through the point of contact is called the normal to the circle.

questions.question: How many tangents can be drawn from a point inside a circle?
questions.answer: No tangent can be drawn from a point inside a circle.

questions.question: How many tangents can be drawn from a point outside a circle?
questions.answer: Two tangents can be drawn from a point outside the circle.

questions.question: State one property of a tangent.
questions.answer: A tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.

questions.question: What is meant by parallel tangents?
questions.answer: Tangents which are parallel to each other and touch the circle on opposite sides.

questions.question: What is the angle between radius and tangent at the point of contact?
questions.answer: The angle is 90 degrees.

questions.question: Can a tangent pass through the interior of a circle?
questions.answer: No, a tangent only touches the circle at one point.

questions.question: What happens when the two points of a secant coincide?
questions.answer: The secant becomes a tangent.

questions.question: What is the centre of a circle?
questions.answer: The fixed point from which all points on the circle are equidistant.

questions.question: What is meant by the length of a tangent?
questions.answer: The distance from the external point to the point of contact.

questions.question: Is every chord a diameter? Give reason.
questions.answer: No, because not all chords pass through the centre.

questions.question: What is meant by concentric circles?
questions.answer: Circles having the same centre but different radii are called concentric circles.

questions.question: Why is the radius shortest distance to tangent?
questions.answer: Because the perpendicular distance is the shortest distance.

questions.question: What is a non-intersecting line with respect to a circle?
questions.answer: A line that does not meet the circle at any point.

questions.question: State one real-life example of tangent.
questions.answer: A wheel touching the ground forms a tangent.

questions.question: What is meant by boundary of a circle?
questions.answer: The curve that encloses the circular region.
